capability_name: Unified intake convergence and safety-gated request promotion
phase_titles: The Foundation Protocol; The Red Flag Gate; Identity and Echoes; The Human Checkpoint
why_it_exists: The product mission depends on one governed intake lineage rather than channel-specific request creation.
scope_statement: Web, NHS App jump-off, phone, secure-link continuation, and support-assisted capture must converge into one SubmissionEnvelope and request-promotion boundary before routine workflow continues.
acceptance_implication: Reject any design that creates a second request pipeline, a phone-only request store, or a channel-specific safety model.

capability_name: Fallback review, degraded receipt, and artifact quarantine
phase_titles: The Foundation Protocol; The Red Flag Gate; The Assurance Ledger
why_it_exists: Accepted progress must never disappear when ingest, safety, or evidence processing degrades.
scope_statement: Unsafe, unreadable, malicious, or unsupported evidence must remain quarantined and route through FallbackReviewCase with same-lineage degraded recovery.
acceptance_implication: Reject any implementation that auto-discards bad evidence, drops accepted submissions silently, or swaps same-shell recovery for a generic error page.
dependency_summary: Binary artifact store (required_baseline); Manual review queue (required_baseline)

capability_name: Identity binding, patient claim, and session authority
phase_titles: The Foundation Protocol; Identity and Echoes
why_it_exists: The canonical request model allows anonymous, partial, and matched-but-unclaimed states, so identity cannot be simplified away.
scope_statement: Patient binding, claim, and writable resume must derive from governed IdentityBinding plus session-establishment decisions instead of ambient login success.
acceptance_implication: Reject any proposal that overwrites patientRef directly, reuses a stale session for claim, or treats authenticated context as proof of writable ownership.
dependency_summary: NHS login rail (required_baseline); Secure-link continuation flow (required_baseline); Telephony identity capture (required_baseline)

capability_name: Authorization, consent, purpose-of-use, and break-glass governance
phase_titles: The Foundation Protocol; Identity and Echoes; The Assurance Ledger
why_it_exists: Vecells must separate identity proof, clinical authorization, consent scope, and exceptional access to keep cross-organisation work lawful and replayable.
scope_statement: RBAC, ABAC, consent scope grants, purpose-of-use tokens, and break-glass review remain baseline control-plane scope for patient, staff, support, hub, and governance surfaces.
acceptance_implication: Reject any architecture that hides authorization in session state, infers consent from prior contact, or omits reason-coded break-glass review from live operations.

capability_name: Auto-merge duplicate requests without governed review
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Human Checkpoint
why_it_exists: Duplicate handling is conservative clustering and review work, not silent merge logic.
scope_statement: Vecells may not auto-merge same-episode or near-duplicate requests without governed duplicate review, attach evidence, and continuity witness proof.
acceptance_implication: Reject any proposal that collapses near-duplicates into a single request without explicit duplicate resolution and replay-safe audit.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: blueprint-init.md#Clinical workspace and operational workflow; phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#3. Non-negotiable invariants; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 05 - No conservative duplicate and same-episode control

capability_name: Direct request workflow-state or closure writes from child domains
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Booking Engine; The Network Horizon; The Pharmacy Loop
why_it_exists: LifecycleCoordinator ownership is one of the hardened cross-phase invariants from the prior reconciliation work.
scope_statement: Booking, hub, pharmacy, triage, and other child domains may emit milestones, blockers, and evidence, but they may not write canonical Request.workflowState or closure directly.
acceptance_implication: Reject any domain design or event handler that maps local success, ambiguity, or reopen semantics straight into request-level workflowState or closure.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: blueprint-init.md#3. The canonical request model; phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#3. Non-negotiable invariants; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 74 - Phase 4 let booking-domain logic write canonical request state directly on success; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 76 - Phase 5 let hub-domain logic write canonical request state directly; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 77 - Phase 6 let pharmacy-domain logic write canonical request state directly on resolve and reopen paths

capability_name: Supplier-specific booking or business logic baked into the core model
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Booking Engine; The Network Horizon; The Pharmacy Loop
why_it_exists: Supplier variability is real, but the core model must remain Vecells-first and adapter-bound.
scope_statement: Supplier-specific search, reservation, booking, directory, dispatch, or transport logic may not be hard-coded into canonical request, queue, or patient-surface semantics.
acceptance_implication: Reject any change that adds vendor names, one-off transport branches, or supplier payload truth directly to core workflow decisions or patient copy.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: blueprint-init.md#1. Product definition; blueprint-init.md#6. Booking and access continuity; platform-runtime-and-release-blueprint.md#AdapterContractProfile; phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#25E. BookingProviderAdapterBinding

capability_name: Countdown or pending UI implying exclusive booking truth
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Booking Engine
why_it_exists: Reservation truth is a hard non-goal guardrail called out by the audit and booking flow.
scope_statement: Countdown timers, pending UI, or offer expiry copy may not imply exclusivity unless ReservationAuthority and CapacityReservation prove a held reservation.
acceptance_implication: Reject any booking or waitlist UI that suggests the slot is held when the current reservation state is only soft-selected, pending, or absent.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#3. Non-negotiable invariants; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 30 - Offer and hold semantics could imply false exclusivity; vecells-complete-end-to-end-flow.md#Booking flow

capability_name: Booked-state reassurance from ambiguous supplier confirmation
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Booking Engine; The Network Horizon
why_it_exists: Booking calmness must remain subordinate to external confirmation truth and practice visibility debt.
scope_statement: Vecells may not imply booked calmness when supplier or hub confirmation remains ambiguous, stale, expired, disputed, or otherwise unresolved by the canonical truth projection.
acceptance_implication: Reject any final patient or staff reassurance that appears before ExternalConfirmationGate clears and the governing truth projection still matches the active tuple.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#3. Non-negotiable invariants; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 31 - Ambiguous supplier responses could still look like success; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 34 - Hub coordination was oversimplified into book or not-book

capability_name: Direct GP record mutation by Vecells inside Pharmacy First flow
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Pharmacy Loop
why_it_exists: The pharmacy boundary is referral, dispatch, and outcome reconciliation, not pretending to be the pharmacy or GP clinical record.
scope_statement: Vecells does not directly mutate GP records as part of Pharmacy First; it composes referral packages, dispatches them through approved rails, and reconciles outcomes back into governed workflow.
acceptance_implication: Reject any pharmacy plan that uses Vecells as the hidden write model for direct GP record updates rather than a referral and reconciliation platform.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: blueprint-init.md#7. Pharmacy First pathway; phase-cards.md#Card 7: Phase 6 - The Pharmacy Loop

capability_name: Mandatory AI gate or autonomous clinical/operational decision-maker
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Human Checkpoint; The Assistive Layer
why_it_exists: Assistive capability is intentionally bounded and may not become the primary decision owner.
scope_statement: AI assistance may not be mandatory for request progression or clinical decisioning, and no assistive output may commit authoritative workflow change without governed human approval.
acceptance_implication: Reject any requirement that blocks ordinary care-path completion on model availability or that authorizes autonomous clinical or operational settlement.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: vecells-complete-end-to-end-flow.md#Baseline invariants; phase-8-the-assistive-layer.md#System after Phase 8; forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 17 - AI assistance was modeled as a mandatory linear stage

capability_name: Treating analytics, assurance, recovery, support, or governance as post-hoc add-ons
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Assurance Ledger
why_it_exists: The corpus explicitly promotes analytics, assurance, recovery, support, and governance into baseline delivery scope.
scope_statement: Analytics, assurance, continuity proof, recovery rehearsal, support, and governance must remain product baseline scope and may not be deferred to a post-launch hardening project.
acceptance_implication: Reject roadmap slicing that declares the core product complete without the control-plane work needed to operate, prove, and recover it.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
source_refs: blueprint-init.md#11. Analytics and assurance; phase-cards.md#Programme Baseline Update (NHS App Deferred); phase-9-the-assurance-ledger.md#Rule 1: evidence comes from the system, not from retrospective narrative

capability_name: Optional PDS enrichment layered onto governed identity binding
phase_titles: Identity and Echoes; Cross-phase
why_it_exists: The baseline identity model is already defined without requiring another external demographic enrichment source.
scope_statement: Any future PDS enrichment must remain optional and additive to IdentityBinding rather than becoming the sole authority for patientRef, claim, or writable resume.
acceptance_implication: A later PDS project belongs in conditional scope and must still preserve verified IdentityBinding as the canonical local authority.

capability_name: Model-vendor-backed assistive rollout cohorts beyond the baseline floor
phase_titles: The Assistive Layer; Cross-phase
why_it_exists: Phase 8 establishes the bounded assistive capability, but broad vendor- and cohort-specific rollout remains conditional on trust, safety, and route fit.
scope_statement: Specific model-vendor-backed assistive cohorts may expand later only through rollout slice contracts, trust envelopes, freeze controls, and independent safety review.
acceptance_implication: A request to widen assistive visibility across tenants, routes, or cohorts belongs in conditional rollout scope, not in the baseline core definition.
dependency_summary: Model vendor contract (feature_flagged); Assistive rollout ladder (required_baseline); Kill switch and freeze controls (required_baseline)

capability_name: Supplier-specific capability expansion hidden behind adapter seams
phase_titles: The Booking Engine; The Network Horizon; The Pharmacy Loop; Cross-phase
why_it_exists: Baseline delivery requires common adapter-bound capability, while richer supplier-specific features remain conditional.
scope_statement: Supplier-specific extras such as richer manage support, additional hold semantics, or provider-specific workflow conveniences belong only behind adapter capability contracts and may expand later without redefining the core model.
acceptance_implication: A request for one supplier-only feature belongs in conditional scope unless the same behavior is normalized into the published adapter contract and does not leak supplier truth into the core domain.
dependency_summary: Supplier capability matrix updates (optional); Supplier sandbox or simulator (replaceable_by_simulator)

capability_name: Direct browser access to adapters or undeclared internal services
phase_titles: Cross-phase; The Foundation Protocol; The Assurance Ledger
why_it_exists: The browser boundary is a published gateway/BFF contract, not a convenience integration surface.
scope_statement: Browsers may talk only to the governed gateway or BFF boundary; they may not call GP, telephony, messaging, pharmacy, MESH, workflow, or raw data services directly.
acceptance_implication: Reject any frontend design or release shortcut that bypasses GatewayBffSurface, AudienceSurfaceRouteContract, or published runtime topology boundaries.
dependency_summary: No external dependency because the capability is intentionally excluded or prohibited.
